description:
cmd_create (src/commands/basic.rs:30) mints a task id BEFORE taking any lock, then takes the per-task lock to save+commit:

    let id = task_id::generate_task_id(&store, &title)?;   // unlocked
    // ...
    let _g = task_lock(&store, &id)?;                       // locks ID 'id'
    store.save_task(&task)?;                                // overwrites bl-<id>.json
    store.commit_task(&id, ...)?;                           // takes state_worktree_flock

generate_task_id is SHA1(title+timestamp)[:id_length], with the default id_length = 4 (16 bits, 65,536 possibilities). It checks store.task_exists(id) to avoid clashes — also unlocked. Two concurrent bl create calls can therefore mint the SAME 4-hex id from different titles (birthday math: 8 workers → ~0.04% per run). The per-task lock then serializes them safely on the same id, but the second writer's save_task overwrites the first writer's JSON file before its own commit_task runs:

  A: mint X, lock(X), save(parallel-4 → bl-X.json), commit, unlock(X)
  B: mint X, lock(X) [blocked], lock(X) [acquired], save(parallel-7 → bl-X.json) OVERWRITES, commit, unlock(X)

Both exit 0. HEAD's bl-X.json holds parallel-7; parallel-4 is gone from disk AND HEAD. Matches the observed test failure shape ('list missing parallel-4', all 8 creates succeeded) and bl-0ca1.

Not a tarpaulin issue. Tarpaulin's coverage instrumentation just widens the mint→commit window enough to surface the race regularly. The same race is reachable in real concurrent use of a shared tracker — two agents minting at the same instant silently lose one task with no error. That's worse than a CI flake; it's data loss without diagnosis.

Fix shape (smallest honest one): turn the unique-id check into 'check, lock, re-check, write' rather than 'check, lock, write'. After acquiring task_lock(id), call store.task_exists(id) again; if true, regenerate the id (bump the timestamp), re-lock the new id, retry. The pure next_unique_id loop in task_id::next_unique_id already encodes the retry; the wrapping needs to be inside the lock acquire/release scope, which means cmd_create owns the loop rather than calling generate_task_id once. Alternative: move id minting under store_lock::state_worktree_flock for the duration of mint+save+commit — heavier but eliminates the seam entirely.

Test: tests/concurrent_writes.rs::parallel_bl_create_across_workers_all_succeed already exercises this; it just passes most of the time. Harden by either reducing id_length in the test fixture (forcing collisions deterministically) or wrapping the existing test in a stress loop (N=1000) under cargo test, so the gate is no longer 'might-fail-under-tarpaulin' but 'always-passes-under-the-stress-knob'.

Supersedes the symptom-only framing of bl-0ca1 (which can close when this lands).
title: Re-check task id under the per-task lock — generate_task_id mint races the commit
